rationale: A batch script (C:\Temp\1.bat) executed via cmd.exe spawned reg.exe twice to write plaintext credentials — the Administrator username (A0, EV-001) and a password (EV-002, EV-003) — into the Winlogon AutoLogon registry keys (HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\DefaultUserName and DefaultPassword). Storing credentials in plaintext in Winlogon is a well-known credential-exposure technique (AutoLogon abuse) that enables persistent or lateral-movement access. The DefaultPassword value was subsequently deleted via regedit.exe (EV-004), which may indicate an attempt to cover tracks after the credentials were harvested or used, though the DefaultUserName value deletion is not evidenced. The activity originates from C:\Temp\ — an atypical working directory for legitimate administrative scripts — under a high-integrity Administrator session, and the batch file origin is unverified; no evidence of a legitimate administrative purpose is present.
